motions.title: Adjusted Ottawa Public Library Budget for 2026
motions.summary: City Council approved an amendment to correct the funding allocation for the Ottawa Public Library's 2026 operating and capital budgets, with no net impact on the overall city budget.
motions.full_text:
Motion No. 2025-71-04
Moved by M. Luloff
Seconded by C. Kitts
WHEREAS the Ottawa Public Library Board approved the 2026 OPL Draft Operating and Capital Budget on December 2, 2025; and
WHEREAS the Finance and Corporate Services Committee (FCSC)
approved the Non-Departmental budget on December 2, 2025; and
WHEREAS the approved Operating Resource Requirement template for the Ottawa Public Library included the correct total gross and net expenditure, but the misalignment of the municipal revenue resulting in a net requirement of $66.730 million versus the correct net requirement of $69.220 million (an additional $2.490 million); and
WHEREAS the approved Operating Resource Requirement template for the Finance and Corporate Services Committee Non-Departmental budget included a net requirement of ($2.343 billion) versus the correct net requirement of ($2.345 billion), (an additional ($2.490 million)); and
WHEREAS the proposed updates will have no net impact on the overall 2026 Draft Operating and Capital Budget;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT Council receive and consider the following updated budget templates to increase the OPL net requirement by $2.490 million and to increase the Non-Departmental net requirement by ($2.490 million) to align the funding between the two templates and ensure the approved resource requirements reflect the correct allocations, with no net financial impact to the overall Draft 2026 Operating and Capital Budget:
- Ottawa Public Library Operating Resource Requirement Template (Revised Page 5 of the Ottawa Public Library Budget Book); and

motions.title: Approval of 2026 Ottawa Police Service Budget
motions.summary: City Council approved the operating and capital budgets for the Ottawa Police Service for 2026, setting funding for public safety and emergency response.
motions.full_text:
The 2026 Ottawa Police Service Draft Operating and Capital Budgets, as recommended by the Ottawa Police Services Board;
For (20): M. Sutcliffe, M. Luloff, L. Dudas, D. Hill, C. Curry, C. Kelly, G. Gower, T. Kavanagh, S. Devine, T. Tierney, S. Plante, J. Leiper, R. Brockington, M. Carr, C. Kitts, I. Skalski, D. Brown, S. Desroches, A. Hubley, and W. Lo
Against (5): L. Johnson, J. Bradley, R. King, A. Troster, and S. Menard
Carried (20 to 5)

motions.title: Increased Funding for Core Infrastructure Projects
motions.summary: City Council allocated an additional $20 million for critical infrastructure like roads, sidewalks, parks, and recreational facilities, funded by assessment growth, and released parkland funds for recreation.
motions.full_text:
Motion No. 2025-71-05
Moved by L. Dudas Seconded by C. Kitts
WHEREAS staff were directed to explore and report back to Council with possible solutions on how the City could invest more in core infrastructure within the 2026 budget prior to final adoption; and
WHEREAS staff have identified an infrastructure funding gap as per the most recent Asset Management Plans approved by Council in 2025, consistent with the experience of municipalities across Canada; and
WHEREAS this gap covers essential assets such as roads, sidewalks, bridges, recreation facilities, and other municipal infrastructure and the infrastructure backlog will require new funding strategies, including debt financing, and possibly external support to close the gap; and
WHEREAS staff have begun work on the Tax Long Range Financial Plan (LRFP) to provide funding strategies to address essential infrastructure needs such as roads and recreational facilities and amenities, in which debt financing is a funding strategy to be leveraged; and
WHEREAS staff will bring forward the Tax LRFP to Committee and Council in the first half of 2026; and
WHEREAS staff have received the final Municipal Property Assessment Corporation (MPAC) roll return; and
WHEREAS staff have advised that they have completed a wholesome review of the final MPAC roll return and have confirmed that the City has slightly exceeded the 2026 budget growth target of 1.6% to 1.65% or approximately $1.2 million in additional annual base funding; and
WHEREAS there are significant Cash-in-lieu of parklands (CILs) funds being held back; and
WHEREAS Legal Services has assessed the holdback percentage and confirmed that up to fifty per cent of the hold back can be released at this time;
THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED THAT Council support the release of CILs as identified by Legal Services for the advancement of recreation infrastructure projects; and
THEREFORE, BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT Council direct staff to increase the draft 2026 capital budget for Tax supported assets with an additional $20 million debenture in which the annual debt servicing of approximately $1.2 million will be funded from the incremental $1.2 million assessment growth funding; and
THEREFORE, IT BE FURTHER RESOLVED THAT staff apply the $20 million capital funding towards core tax supported infrastructure such as roads, sidewalks, parks and recreational facilities.

motions.title: Approval of Rural Affairs Committee Capital Program
motions.summary: City Council approved the 2026 Capital Program for the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee, funding specific projects in Ottawa's rural communities.
motions.full_text:
Motion No. 2025-71-06
Moved by D. Brown Seconded by I. Skalski
WHEREAS the 2026 Draft Capital Budget for the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee was rejected at the meeting of December 4, 2025 with three dissents;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that Council, sitting as Committee of the Whole, approve the Agriculture and Rural Affairs Committee Capital Program (page 5) [individual projects listed pages 10-18], including revised page 17 of the ARAC budget book.

motions.title: Permanent Funding for Free Youth Recreation Program
motions.summary: The 'Play Free' program, which offers free recreational activities for children and youth across the city, received permanent base funding of $550,000.
motions.full_text:
Motion No. 2025-71-07
Moved by I. Skalski
Seconded by M. Sutcliffe
WHEREAS the City of Ottawa is committed to promoting healthy lifestyles and physical activity among children and youth; and
WHEREAS the Play Free program is a City initiative offering free access to recreational activities such as swimming, skating, and gym sports every Saturday throughout 2023- 2025; and
WHEREAS the program encourages participation by children and youth aged 17 and under, along with accompanying adults, and includes a variety of drop-in sports such as badminton, basketball, soccer, and volleyball; and
WHEREAS the Play Free program aligns with the City's goals of fostering community engagement, accessibility, and wellness;
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED THAT Ottawa City Council allocate $550,000 of base funding to make this program a permanent program moving forward and to:
1. Endorse and promote the Play Free program as a key initiative to encourage physical activity and family engagement across the city;
2. Direct staff to ensure clear communication and outreach regarding program details, including eligibility, and participating locations;
3. Encourage partnerships with community organizations and schools to maximize awareness and participation; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED THAT the funding source for these allocations be the $550,000 in projected savings from the City
